title: Key idea: uniqueness of OLS via normal equation
text: A unique OLS solution from:\n$$(X**{T}X)**{-1}X**{T}y$$\nrequires X to have full column rank (equivalently, XᵀX invertible).

title: One-hot encoding pitfall
text: If you include all one-hot columns plus an intercept, one column becomes redundant (dummy-variable trap) → columns linearly dependent.

title: Decide this statement
text: Ask: does this statement imply (or incorrectly claim) lack of full column rank / multicollinearity?
